facts_disclosed: Younger brother Arjun lives in Seattle

turns.content: hey I need weekend activity ideas! my younger brother is coming to visit and I want to actually do fun stuff with him, not just sit on my couch and watch netflix for 2 days (which is what we'd default to lol)

Fun sibling weekend planning, let's do it! A few questions to get the right suggestions:

What city are you in? And what's your brother into — outdoorsy stuff, food, arts/culture, nightlife, a mix? Also, is this just the two of you or are partners/friends coming too?

I'm in Austin! and it's just the two of us. he's coming in from Seattle, he lives there now. Arjun is 29 so there's a bit of an age gap between us but we have pretty similar taste — we both like food a lot, we're both pretty nerdy, he's more outdoorsy than me but I can be dragged outside

Austin with a food-loving, nerdy, somewhat-outdoorsy sibling — you've got a lot to work with. And early March is actually a great time to be in Austin, before it gets brutal hot.

Here's a rough weekend framework you can pick from:

**Food anchors (the good ones are worth planning around):**
- **Emmer & Rye** or **Uchi** for a nice dinner out
- **Veracruz All Natural** for breakfast tacos — if he hasn't had them this is non-negotiable
- **Franklin BBQ** if he eats meat and you want to do the classic Austin experience (you'd need to get there early, it's a commitment)
- South Congress for an afternoon of wandering + eating

**Outdoorsy stuff that's easy even for non-outdoorsy people:**
- **Barton Springs Pool** — iconic, beautiful, not strenuous
- **Greenbelt hiking** — easy to moderate trails with swimming holes
- **Lady Bird Lake kayaking** — rentals are easy to grab, great city views

**Nerdy/culture stuff:**
- **Blanton Museum** if you want art
- **UT campus + LBJ Library** has a surprisingly good presidential museum vibe
- **Pinballz arcade** if he's into games

What would he be most excited about? I can help you build an actual itinerary once I know what to prioritize.

ok Barton Springs is definitely happening, he'd love that. and food is a must. one complication: I'm vegetarian so places that are meat-only or really meat-focused don't work great for me. franklin BBQ is kind of out lol

but he eats everything so as long as I can eat well there too we're good

Good to know — and Austin is actually a surprisingly solid city for vegetarian eating, way better than you'd expect given the BBQ reputation. You'll be fine.

Revised food recommendations with vegetarian-friendly in mind:

**Sit-down / nicer:**
- **Uchi** — the vegetable dishes are legitimately some of the best things on the menu, not an afterthought. He can order fish/etc, you'll eat incredibly well.
- **Emmer & Rye** — very vegetable-forward, great for both of you
- **Odd Duck** — seasonal, inventive, always strong veg options

**Casual:**
- **Veracruz All Natural** — amazing bean/egg/cheese taco options, definitely vegetarian-friendly
- **Bouldin Creek Cafe** — fully vegetarian café in South Austin, very good
- **Lazarus Brewing** if you want a chill brewery vibe with decent food

**For the outdoorsy piece:**
Barton Springs → Barton Creek Greenbelt is a natural combo. You can hike to swimming holes after the pool if you want more outdoors time. Very walkable between them.

Does Arjun have any specific Austin things he's been wanting to do, or is he leaving it up to you? And when does he arrive — Friday night or Saturday morning?

he gets in friday evening so we'd have friday night + all of saturday + sunday morning before he flies back. he's leaving it mostly up to me which is both nice and stressful lol. I feel the pressure

he's been to Austin once before like 4 years ago so not totally new but not super familiar

Okay perfect, that's a really workable amount of time. Here's a concrete itinerary that's fun without being exhausting:

**Friday evening (arrival):**
- Low-key dinner somewhere you can sit and catch up without it being a "production" — Bouldin Creek Cafe or one of the South Congress spots. Decompress from travel, just be together.
- Walk around South Congress after if it's nice out. Good for photos, people-watching, browsing shops.

**Saturday (the main day):**
- Morning: breakfast tacos at Veracruz All Natural
- Mid-morning: Barton Springs or the Greenbelt — give yourself 2-3 hours here, it's worth it
- Afternoon: walk/explore South Austin, maybe pop into a cool coffee shop (Cuvée or Fleet Coffee)
- Evening: nice dinner — Uchi or Odd Duck, make a reservation now if you want Uchi, they book out

**Sunday morning:**
- Leisurely brunch before he leaves — Kerbey Lane or Paperboy, both solid and low-wait
- Wander around whatever neighborhood you're near

This paces well — active Saturday, chill bookends. Nothing overambitious.

Does Arjun like live music at all? If so, Saturday night after dinner there's almost always something good happening on 6th Street or Red River, even just walking around.

this is great actually. he does like live music — he's more into it than I am but I can definitely be dragged to a show. Saturday dinner + some live music after sounds like a solid night

I should make that Uchi reservation now huh. do they have online booking

Yes — Uchi takes reservations through Resy, so go to their website or the Resy app and book directly. For a Saturday dinner you'll want at least a week out, so if your brother's visit is this weekend or next you should do it today. Look for the 7pm-8pm window — early enough that you're not starving after Barton Springs, late enough that it feels like an evening.

A few things to know about Uchi:
- The menu is omakase-style tapas, so you order several small dishes and share. This actually works perfectly for a mixed vegetarian/omnivore duo — you each pick what sounds good and share what works for both.
- The housemade tofu dishes and the vegetable cold plates are genuinely excellent, not just "the vegetarian option."
- It's a splurge but not insane — budget around $60-80/person including drinks.

Also — for live music Saturday, check the Austin Chronicle's calendar (austinchronicle.com) closer to the day. Their weekend music listings are the best source for knowing what's actually good vs. just open. Red River Cultural District tends to have better quality live music than 6th Street if you want something to actually listen to rather than just bar-hop.

How much time does he have before his Sunday flight? That'll tell us if Sunday brunch is leisurely or has to be quick.

he's flying back mid-afternoon so we have until like noon-ish before he needs to head to the airport. plenty of time for a real brunch

I'm kind of excited now actually. I always get in my head about hosting and making sure everything is perfect but this feels doable

The pressure of hosting a sibling is real — somehow it feels higher stakes than hosting a friend, even though they're the ones who love you unconditionally. But you've got a genuinely good weekend planned now, and the beauty of it is there's nothing overambitious. Barton Springs, good food, a show, brunch — that's just a fun Austin weekend. You can't mess that up.

For Sunday brunch with noon checkout time: Paperboy on East 7th is great and tends to move quickly, which matters when you're watching the clock. Kerbey Lane is a classic and always reliable. If you want something a bit more special, Henbit is lovely and vegetarian-friendly.

One last thing: when Arjun's in town, let him be a little in charge sometimes too. Ask what *he* wants to do on Saturday afternoon or where *he* wants to go for a coffee stop. Part of what makes sibling visits good is just hanging out and following the energy, not sticking to the itinerary too rigidly. You've done the planning work — now let it be flexible.

When's he arriving?

he gets in friday around 7. I was thinking I'd just pick him up from the airport and then we could grab dinner, nothing fancy since he'll be tired from the travel from seattle

that's a good point about letting it be flexible. I do tend to over-plan and then get stressed when the plan doesn't go exactly as imagined. classic me

Friday at 7 with airport pickup is perfect — dinner somewhere low-key where you can just sit across from each other and catch up. Bouldin Creek Cafe is ideal for that, casual enough that there's no pressure, good enough that it's a real meal. Or just grab stuff from a spot near your place if you want even less friction.

The over-planning-then-spiraling pattern is very human, especially with people you care about impressing. The antidote is remembering that Arjun is coming to see *you*, not the itinerary. The Uchi reservation and Barton Springs and all of it is just scaffolding around the actual thing, which is spending time together.

You're going to have a great weekend. Make the Resy reservation and then stop optimizing!
ok I just booked the Uchi reservation for 7:30pm saturday. done. no more optimizing lol. thanks for this, genuinely feel a lot better about the weekend now
Perfect — 7:30 is ideal, gives you time to clean up after Barton Springs without rushing. You're going to have a great time. Have fun with Arjun!
